summary: dcchbot ships hardcoded author backdoor with remote code execution and token exfiltration
details: dcchbot/main.py defines CODER_ID = 1317800611441283139 and grants that Discord user ID owner-equivalent privileges across multiple slash commands on every installed instance of the bot. The /token command returns bot._token (the installer's Discord bot token) in chat to the hardcoded author ID; /bot-update executes os.system(f'pip install dcchbot=={latest_version}') followed by os.system('dcchbot') on the installer's host; /op grants Discord administrator role on the installer's guild; /stop shuts the bot down. The installer has no way to revoke this backdoor short of forking and editing the source. This is a persistent, author-controlled RCE and credential-exfiltration channel against anyone who deploys this package. Additionally, dcchbot/__init__.py invokes run() at import time, which blocks on input() and starts a Discord bot thread — an aggressive import-time side effect that breaks any tool that merely imports the package. A hardcoded RFC1918 version-check URL (10.112.101.32:194) is also present, which on the author's network could be used to spoof update responses and trigger the os.system update path.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
